reason: Response A delivers a single clean, self-contained ironic reversal that subverts the standard "fresh start" trope without over-explaining, which matches Sonnet's tendency toward economical, dry wit. Response B leans on the well-worn "done is better than perfect" cliche and then tacks on an anthropomorphized punchline (perfect "never returns your calls") that feels more like a model straining to demonstrate cleverness. The tighter, more original construction of A reads as the genuine Sonnet output, though the distinction is subtle.
